test_title: Seoul Neighborhood Match for Digital Nomads

questions.question: How do you prefer to start your productive morning?

options.text: In a sleek, high-rise coworking space with a city view.
Grabbing a specialty coffee in a converted warehouse or industrial loft.
A quiet walk through a sophisticated, hilly residential village.
In a vibrant cafe surrounded by students and street art.

questions.question: What kind of 'after-work' vibe are you looking for?

options.text: Exploring hidden 'Newtro' (new + retro) bars in narrow alleys.
A world-class dining experience or a wine bar with an international crowd.
Live indie music, street performances, and affordable drinks.
High-end shopping followed by a gym session in a luxury complex.

questions.question: Which aesthetic inspires your creativity the most?

options.text: Raw concrete, exposed brick, and artisanal craftsmanship.
Polished glass, neon lights, and futuristic architecture.
Minimalist luxury, art galleries, and quiet greenery.
DIY posters, colorful murals, and quirky vintage shops.

questions.question: What is your priority when choosing a 'work-from-anywhere' spot?

options.text: A community of tech entrepreneurs and fast-paced networking.
A place where I can easily blend in as an expat or English speaker.
Affordability and a high density of 24-hour options.
An authentic local experience that feels like 'Old Seoul'.

questions.question: How do you prefer to spend your Saturday afternoon?

options.text: Picnicking in a large forest park right next to trendy pop-up stores.
Browsing designer boutiques and visiting a private museum.
Hunting for unique stationery and vintage clothes in crowded markets.
Attending a global tech conference or visiting a massive underground mall.
A healthy grain bowl or trendy brunch in a minimalist cafe.
Classic Korean street food or spicy rice cakes on a budget.
A quick, efficient meal at a high-end salad bar near the office.
A steaming bowl of noodles in a gritty, traditional local market.

questions.question: What is your preferred mode of transportation around the neighborhood?

options.text: Walking through green parks and pedestrian-friendly streets.
Using a sleek electric scooter or the ultra-efficient subway lines.
Taxis or private cars to avoid the hills and maintain privacy.
I don't mind getting lost in a maze of winding alleyways on foot.

Seongsu.vibe: The 'Brooklyn of Seoul'. An industrial district turned creative hub where red-brick factories house tech startups and world-class cafes.
Seongsu.nomad_tip: Safety/Internet: Exceptionally safe with many 'work-friendly' cafes. Wi-Fi is blazing fast, and the community is full of young entrepreneurs.

Hannam.vibe: Sophisticated, global, and hilly. This is where international diplomats and celebrities live, offering a refined blend of art galleries and boutique dining.
Hannam.nomad_tip: Safety/Internet: Very quiet and prestigious. Internet is reliable, but many cafes are 'Instagram-focused' and might have limited seating for long laptop sessions.

Hongdae.vibe: Youthful, rebellious, and artistic. Home to indie music, street fashion, and a 24/7 energy that never sleeps.
Hongdae.nomad_tip: Safety/Internet: Great for budget-conscious nomads. Countless 24-hour cafes with solid Wi-Fi. It can be loud, so noise-canceling headphones are a must.

Euljiro.vibe: Where the 1970s meet 2026. A gritty maze of printing shops and hardware stores that hide the city's trendiest speakeasies and retro cafes.
Euljiro.nomad_tip: Safety/Internet: Authentic but can be chaotic. Internet is generally good, but 'hidden' spots might have spotty signals. Perfect for those who want to avoid tourists.

Gangnam.vibe: The beating heart of modern Korea. High-speed, high-tech, and high-fashion. Everything is polished, efficient, and professional.
Gangnam.nomad_tip: Safety/Internet: The best coworking infrastructure in Seoul. Public Wi-Fi is everywhere. It’s corporate and busy, so expect a 'hustle' culture.
